l1: Are all members in the replication group reporting the backlog or sync issues?
When did files stop syncing between the target replication partners?
Is the replication failure affecting the SYSVOL share or data volumes?
l2: Have any of the replication group members recently suffered a dirty shutdown?
Is the DFSR database corrupted or reporting event ID 2213 or 4012?
Are firewalls blocking RPC ports dynamic range 49152-65535 between partners?

l1: Run 'dfsrdiag backlog /rgname:<group> /rfname:<folder> /sme:<source> /rme:<dest>' to check queue size.
Check the DFS Replication event log for Warning or Error event entries.
Run 'wmic /namespace:\\root\microsoftdfs path dfsrreplicatedfolderinfo get replicationgroupname,replicatedfoldername,state' to see folder status.
l2: Run 'dfsrdiag replicationstate' to dump the current active transactions.
Generate a health report using 'dfsrdiag healthreport /rgname:<name> /path:<output>'.
Check staging folder usage via 'wmic /namespace:\\root\microsoftdfs path dfsrlocalmemberinfo get stagingspaceinuse'.

l1: Restart the DFS Replication service on the affected member.
Verify network connectivity and RPC port availability between partners.
Clear up disk space if the staging folder or volume is hitting full thresholds.
l2: Resume replication after a dirty shutdown by running the specific WMI method for ID 2213.
Perform a non-authoritative synchronization rebuild by altering the msDFSR-Enabled attribute.
Adjust the staging quota limits upward if large files are blocking the pipeline.

l1: Export the complete DFS Replication event log into an .evtx package.
Collect the generated HTML health report from dfsrdiag utility.
Gather 'dfsrdiag dumpguid' and basic system storage metrics logs.
l2: Collect complete DFSR debug logs located under the %SystemRoot%\Debug folder.
Export the registry configuration keys for the DFSR service parameters.
Run a continuous packet trace during forced sync attempts using built-in netsh.

dirty_shutdown_recovery.title: DFSR Replication Stopped due to Dirty Shutdown (Event ID 2213)

steps.expect: Find recent log entries confirming a dirty shutdown was detected

steps.action: wmic /namespace:\\root\microsoftdfs path dfsrvolumeconfig get volume guid, diskspaceinuse
steps.expect: Identify the specific database volume GUID that experienced the shutdown

steps.action: wmic /namespace:\\root\microsoftdfs path dfsrvolumeconfig where volumeGuid="<GUID>" call ResumeReplication
steps.expect: Method returns ReturnValue = 0 indicating command success
Look for Event ID 2214 confirming connection is successfully re-established
